title: Agentic Workflows Just Changed AI Automation Forever! (Claude Code)

extracted.hook: AI automation is about to change forever.

accelerates: "So now that we've talked about those big shifts and you've seen me build one right in front of you guys, let's talk about what the future's going to look like with this stuff."
slows: "Now something else I think is really cool is that you can have multiple agents running simultaneously. [music]"
"All right. Fourth is instant API and MCP integrations. So if you've built workflows in Naden, you know the pain of API authentication, headers, JSON bodies, query parameters."

analogies: "So you explain where the data is coming from, what needs to happen, and then where it should end up. The agent then figures out the rest. So just think of it like you're hiring an actual human developer"

specificity: 25% of enterprises using generative AI will deploy agentic pilots this year, jumping to 50% by 2027.
The AI agent market is projected to grow from roughly 8 billion in 2025 to high 40 or low 50 billion range by 2030, which is a 43% compound annual growth rate.
frameworks: "So the first one is self-healing. This is probably the biggest and one of the coolest ones because if you build something in Nitn when the workflow breaks you read the error message you tweak the node you test it again and if it breaks again you repeat."
